judgment.summary: Exceptional revenue growth and strong margins are offset by a high trailing P/E, though the forward P/E is very low.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 59.7%; next earnings date 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue growth 251.0% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Operating margin 70.0% and Profit margin 34.2% — industry-leading profitability.
Return on equity 39.3% and Return on assets 22.8% — highly efficient capital deployment.
Forward P/E 8.22x — suggests significant earnings growth is expected.
Free cash flow $2.26B TTM — strong cash generation.
negatives: Trailing P/E 49.31x — valuation appears stretched on historical earnings.
Debt/Equity 1.50 — leverage is notable, though offset by strong cash flow.
Quarterly earnings growth and overall earnings growth figures are not provided, making it hard to gauge recent trends.
Analyst growth estimates are extremely high, indicating potential for significant misses if targets are not met.

news.paragraph: Jim Cramer expressed caution on Sandisk, suggesting the stock needs to decline further before it becomes an attractive investment. This sentiment follows recent headlines where he also voiced concerns about Seagate and Western Digital being too pricey. While Sandisk recently posted a strong earnings surprise, Cramer's commentary introduces a note of caution for traders considering new positions.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer on Sandisk: “Gotta Let It Come Down More”
Jim Cramer on Western Digital: “That Seems Too Rich to Me”
